title: Solving Equations of the type $|f(x)| = g(x)$ — PYP Mastery

questions.question: Find the coordinates of the points of intersection of the graphs $y = |3x - 2|$ and $y = |x + 6|$.
questions.explanation:
Case 1: $3x-2 = x+6 \implies 2x=8, x=4, y=10$. Case 2: $3x-2 = -x-6 \implies 4x=-4, x=-1, y=5$.

**Common mistake:** Arithmetic slips in Case 2 or neglecting to find the y-coordinates.

questions.question: Solve the equation $|2x + 5| = |x - 1|$.
questions.explanation:
Case 1: $2x+5 = x-1 \implies x=-6$. Case 2: $2x+5 = -x+1 \implies 3x=-4 \implies x = -4/3 \approx -1.33$.

**Common mistake:** Sign errors resulting in $x = -4$ or incorrectly handling the division of -4 by 3.

questions.question: Solve the equation $|4x - 1| = |2x + 9|$.
questions.explanation:
Case 1: $4x-1 = 2x+9 \implies 2x=10, x=5$. Case 2: $4x-1 = -2x-9 \implies 6x=-8 \implies x = -4/3 \approx -1.33$.

**Common mistake:** Incorrectly expanding Case 2 as $-2x+9$ or miscalculating the root $x=5$.

questions.question: Find the values of x for which $|x - a| = |x - b|$ where a and b are distinct constants.
questions.explanation:
Case 1: $x-a = x-b$. So $x-a = -(x-b) \implies x-a = -x+b \implies $x$ = a+b \implies x = (a+b)/2$.

**Common mistake:** Thinking that no solution exists because Case 1 fails, missing the geometric midpoint property of the second case.

questions.question: Solve $|5x + 3| = |2x - 15|$.
questions.explanation:
Case 1: $5x+3 = 2x-15 \implies 3x=-18, x=-6$. Case 2: $5x+3 = -2x+15 \implies 7x=12 \implies x = 12/7 \approx 1.71$.

**Common mistake:** Arithmetic slips when dividing 18 by 3 or 12 by 7, or sign errors in the case expansions.
